title: IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference)
description: Without knowing the application's authorization mechanisms, there's a risk of IDOR vulnerabilities. An attacker could manipulate URLs or other parameters to access resources or perform actions that they are not authorized to perform, potentially gaining elevated privileges or accessing sensitive data.
attack_vector: Directly referencing internal objects (e.g., database IDs, file names) in URLs or request parameters without proper authorization checks.
mitigations: Implement robust authorization checks for all API endpoints and web pages.
Use indirect object references (e.g., GUIDs) instead of direct object references.
Regularly review and update authorization logic to address new vulnerabilities.
